claims.claim: It provides practical harm-reduction guidance on drug and dietary interactions.
claims.notes: Extracted from Benjamin-Malcolm-PharmD-MPH-2023-Ayahuasca-and-Drug-Interaction using rule-based keyword matching.

claims.claim: --- ## Key Claims ### Claim 1 - claim: Harmala alkaloids in ayahuasca act as reversible MAO-A inhibitors enabling oral DMT bioavailability - type: mechanism - entities: [DMT, MAOI, harmine, harmaline, tetrahydroharmine] - directionality: increases bioavailability - confidence: high - evidence_strength: moderate ### Claim 2 - claim: Combining MAO-A inhibitors with serotonergic drugs can cause serotonin syndrome - type: contraindication - entities: [MAOI, SSRIs, serotonergic drugs] - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong ### Claim 3 - claim: Combining MAO-A inhibitors with dietary tyramine can cause hypertensive crisis - type: contraindication - entities: [MAOI, tyramine] - directionality: increases blood pressure - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong ### Claim 4 - claim: SSRIs, SNRIs, stimulants, and certain opioids are contraindicated with ayahuasca - type: contraindication - entities: [ayahuasca, SSRIs, SNRIs, stimulants, opioids] - confidence: high - evidence_strength: moderate ### Claim 5 - claim: Antipsychotics and SSRIs can attenuate psychedelic effects through receptor-level interactions - type: interaction - entities: [psychedelics, antipsychotics, SSRIs] - directionality: decreases effect - confidence: medium - evidence_strength: moderate ### Claim 6 - claim: Cardiovascular effects of ayahuasca include increases in blood pressure and heart rate but are generally lower than IV DMT - type: risk - entities: [ayahuasca, DMT] - directionality: increases BP/HR - confidence: medium - evidence_strength: moderate ### Claim 7 - claim: Drug washout periods should be based on at least five half-lives prior to ayahuasca ingestion - type: guidance - entities: [ayahuasca, medications] - confidence: high - evidence_strength: moderate --- ## Mechanisms Described - Reversible MAO-A inhibition - Serotonergic toxicity (excess 5-HT signaling) - Tyramine-induced hypertensive response - Sympathomimetic cardiovascular effects - Pharmacokinetic washout (half-life dependent) --- ## Clinical / Practical Guidance - Avoid serotonergic drugs prior to ayahuasca - Avoid tyramine-rich foods when MAO inhibition is present - Use ≥5 half-life rule for medication washout [oai_citation:0‡Benjamin-Malcolm-PharmD-MPH-2023-Ayahuasca and Drug Interaction.pdf](sediment://file_00000000e0d871f4b71d3c3ac377b796) - Screen for psychiatric risk (e.g.

claims.claim: psychosis, bipolar disorder) - Monitor cardiovascular responses during use --- ## Contraindications / Risks - Serotonin syndrome (MAOI + serotonergic drugs) [oai_citation:1‡Benjamin-Malcolm-PharmD-MPH-2023-Ayahuasca and Drug Interaction.pdf](sediment://file_00000000e0d871f4b71d3c3ac377b796) - Hypertensive crisis (MAOI + tyramine) [oai_citation:2‡Benjamin-Malcolm-PharmD-MPH-2023-Ayahuasca and Drug Interaction.pdf](sediment://file_00000000e0d871f4b71d3c3ac377b796) - Cardiovascular instability - Psychiatric destabilization (psychosis, mania) - Drug–drug interaction toxicity --- ## Interaction Relevance - MAOI + SSRIs → serotonin syndrome risk - MAOI + stimulants → hypertensive risk - Ayahuasca + antidepressants → contraindicated - Ayahuasca + tyramine → hypertensive crisis - Psychedelics + antipsychotics → attenuation --- ## Limitations - Not peer-reviewed - Derived from slide presentation format - Selective synthesis of literature - Potential bias due to clinical/consulting role --- ## Notes This is a high-value applied pharmacology source but should be treated as secondary synthesis rather than primary evidence.
